1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم إذا السماء انشقت
1.english: When the sky has split [open]
And has responded to its Lord and was obligated [to do so]
1.transliteration: Wa azinat li Rabbihaa wa huqqat

1.english: And when the earth has been extended
And has cast out that within it and relinquished [it]
1.transliteration: Wa alqat maa feehaa wa takhallat

1.english: And has responded to its Lord and was obligated [to do so] -
1.transliteration: Wa azinat li Rabbihaa wa huqqat

1.arabic: يا أيها الإنسان إنك كادح إلى ربك كدحا فملاقيه
1.english: O mankind, indeed you are laboring toward your Lord with [great] exertion and will meet it.
1.transliteration: Yaaa ayyuhal insaanu innaka kaadihun ilaa Rabbika kad han famulaaqeeh

1.english: Then as for he who is given his record in his right hand,
1.transliteration: Fa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo biyameenih

1.english: He will be judged with an easy account
And return to his people in happiness.

1.arabic: وأما من أوتي كتابه وراء ظهره
1.english: But as for he who is given his record behind his back,
1.transliteration: Wa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo waraaa'a zahrih

1.english: He will cry out for destruction
And [enter to] burn in a Blaze.
Indeed, he had [once] been among his people in happiness;
Indeed, he had thought he would never return [to Allah].

1.arabic: بلى إن ربه كان به بصيرا
1.english: But yes! Indeed, his Lord was ever of him, Seeing.
1.transliteration: Balaaa inna Rabbahoo kaana bihee baseeraa

1.english: So I swear by the twilight glow
And [by] the night and what it envelops
And [by] the moon when it becomes full
[That] you will surely experience state after state.
So what is [the matter] with them [that] they do not believe,

1.arabic: وإذا قرئ عليهم القرآن لا يسجدون ۩
1.english: And when the Qur'an is recited to them, they do not prostrate [to Allah]?
1.transliteration: Wa izaa quri'a 'alaihimul Quraanu laa yasjudoon

1.english: But those who have disbelieved deny,
And Allah is most knowing of what they keep within themselves.
So give them tidings of a painful punishment,

1.arabic: إلا الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات لهم أجر غير ممنون
1.english: Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them is a reward uninterrupted.
1.transliteration: Illal lazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati lahum ajrun ghairu mamnoon
